# Population modeling: a systems approach

# Population modeling: a systems approach

### Authors

## Abstract

A single species population dynamics model based on the functional representations of birth, growth, and death has been constructed. Laboratory parameter estimates were used in a computer implementation of the model to simulate field populations. Preliminary results of replicate runs with parametric sampling indicated reasonable statistical agreement. Further development with the systems analysis technique is planned.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Mathematical models analyze population changes, influencing conservation strategies and resource management, as highlighted in studies on adaptation and ecological dynamics.
Mathematical physics aids in developing models that predict disease spread, enabling effective public health interventions, as seen in recent research on population and within-host dynamics.
Biomathematical approaches quantify ecological impacts of human activities, supporting sustainable practices and policy-making, as demonstrated in comprehensive population dynamics studies.
